mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term market repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative following INDPRO contractions, or the signal performs identically across supply-constrained and demand-driven regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital for corporate debt refinancing rises sharply, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive or flat when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration, which suppresses long-term earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE increases simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during high inflation periods signals resilient economic activity that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises during high inflation, or the regime-specific signal shows no out-of-sample predictive power.
